title: Mobile Heavy Equipment Mechanics, Except Engines
description: Diagnose, adjust, repair, or overhaul mobile mechanical, hydraulic, and pneumatic equipment, such as cranes, bulldozers, graders, and conveyors, used in construction, logging, and mining.

tasks.text: Repair and replace damaged or worn parts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test mechanical products and equipment after repair or assembly to ensure proper performance and compliance with manufacturers' specifications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate and inspect machines or heavy equipment to diagnose defects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read and understand operating manuals, blueprints, and technical drawings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dismantle and reassemble heavy equipment using hoists and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Overhaul and test machines or equipment to ensure operating efficiency.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust, maintain, and repair or replace subassemblies, such as transmissions and crawler heads, using hand tools, jacks, and cranes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair, rewire, and troubleshoot electrical systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs, using engine diagnostic equipment such as computerized test equipment and calibration devices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine parts for damage or excessive wear, using micrometers and gauges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Weld or solder broken parts and structural members, using electric or gas welders and soldering tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Research, order, and maintain parts inventory for services and repairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fit bearings to adjust, repair, or overhaul mobile mechanical, hydraulic, and pneumatic equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Schedule maintenance for industrial machines and equipment, and keep equipment service records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Clean, lubricate, and perform other routine maintenance work on equipment and vehicles.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assemble gear systems, and align frames and gears.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean parts by spraying them with grease solvent or immersing them in tanks of solvent.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust and maintain industrial machinery, using control and regulating devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fabricate needed parts or items from sheet metal.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct workers who are assembling or disassembling equipment or cleaning parts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
